quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an implemented LLM tournament that ranks candidates on literature-based rationale and profile.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Reports concordance with experts and intra-rater consistency for this exact judge channel.

quotes.quote: Given a disease, Robin automatically identifies relevant in vitro assays that model key disease mechanisms and proposes specific drug candidates to evaluate in these experimental models.
quotes.rationale: Shows the ranked candidates feed selection of what is proposed for experimental evaluation.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Experimental data analysis is routed back into generating the next round of candidates, i.e. closed feedback.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Shows hybrid human/automated substrate in the analysis of physical assay data.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: A distinct human-performed analysis step whose downstream routing is not specified.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
quotes.rationale: Humans execute experiments and mediate the data handed to the system.

quotes.quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
quotes.rationale: The source states outlines are not executable protocols; no validator of protocol executability is reported.

diagnostics.consequence: Executability of proposed experiments is unchecked within the loop, so failures surface only after human reinterpretation at the bench.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

diagnostics.statement: No validator of protocol executability is reported; the system produces experimental outlines rather than executable protocols, requiring human translation before laboratory execution.

diagnostics.consequence: Ranking quality is credentialed only by concordance with human preference, so shared expert-and-model bias could propagate into which candidates reach costly assays.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

diagnostics.statement: The reliability evidence for the LLM judge is agreement and intra-rater consistency with expert preferences, not validation of the ranking against physical assay outcomes.

diagnostics.consequence: The degree of computational closure cannot be determined, since human judgment may be silently mediating what the system treats as experimental evidence.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

diagnostics.statement: Routing of human-performed analyses (read alignment, flow cytometry) into the candidate-generation loop is described inconsistently: some analysis is autonomous and feeds the next round, while other steps are human-performed with unspecified downstream use.

diagnostics.consequence: Iteration across rounds cannot be audited or reproduced, and human gatekeeping over round-to-round escalation is untraceable.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

diagnostics.statement: No memory structure or policy-authority arrangement is reported for how prior rounds' results are retained or who authorizes the next round.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report a validation of the LLM tournament ranking against downstream physical assay outcomes rather than only against expert preference agreement, stating how many top-ranked candidates showed measured activity.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Top-ranked candidates show a statistically significant higher assay hit rate than lower-ranked or randomly selected candidates
success_check.evidence_needed: Assay results tabulated against tournament rank position with a significance test
success_check.observable: Reported hit rate of top-ranked versus lower-ranked candidates in the in vitro assays

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

recommendations.proposed_action: Document step by step which analysis outputs are produced by humans versus automated components and exactly which of them are fed back into the next round of candidate generation.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every reported analysis step has an explicit actor and an explicit statement of whether it enters the next candidate round
success_check.evidence_needed: Published pipeline description or trace log covering all reported rounds
success_check.observable: A per-step provenance table naming actor and downstream consumer for every analysis output

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add and report an explicit executability check on generated experimental outlines before they are handed to bench scientists, recording how much human translation each outline required.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reported for every outline used, with the modification rate quantified rather than described qualitatively
success_check.evidence_needed: Logged comparison of generated outlines against the protocols actually executed
success_check.observable: Proportion of generated outlines executed at the bench without human modification

policy_type: LLM-judged pairwise tournament ranking over literature-grounded candidates
iterative human-in-the-loop experimental round scheduling driven by assay results
